name: Melasma
dek: The most-misunderstood pigment disorder we see. Hormone-sensitive, UV-driven, recurrence-prone. This is the protocol — molecules, products, routine, supplements, and what to avoid — for keeping it dormant.
oneliner: Symmetric, hormone-driven pigment. Controllable, not curable.

triggers.n: The single biggest driver. Visible-light contribution is real but secondary.
Pregnancy, OCPs, HRT. Suppress the trigger, the pigment fades slowly.
Cooking, hot yoga, steam. Underestimated in Indian kitchens.
Acne, eczema, post-procedure. PIH layered on melasma confounds diagnosis.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic acid
ingredients.evidence: Multiple RCTs · oral & topical · gold-standard adjunct

ingredients.name: Azelaic acid 15–20%
ingredients.evidence: RCTs comparable to hydroquinone 4% in some trials

ingredients.name: Hydroquinone 2–4% (Rx)
ingredients.evidence: The reference. Cycle 12 weeks on, off. Rx in India.

ingredients.name: Kojic acid 1–2%
ingredients.evidence: Modest data · sensitisation risk in Indian skin

ingredients.name: Glutathione (topical)
ingredients.evidence: Largely marketing. Oral / IV is a different conversation.

products.name: Anthelios UVMune 400 Invisible Fluid SPF 50+
products.why: Highest documented UVA-PF — the floor of any melasma plan.

products.name: Tranexamic 03%
products.why: Real concentration at evidence-aligned dose. Indian-brand standout.

products.name: Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.why: Cheapest serious azelaic in India. Twice-daily for 12 weeks.

products.name: Tretinoin 0.05% Cream (Rx)
products.why: Cleaner vehicle for night use; warrants the price for severe cases.

avoid.b: TikTok recipes featuring lemon, mercury creams, or steroid-laced 'fairness' creams. They damage faster than they fade.
Q-switched lasers can rebound melasma. Always trial a topical protocol for 12+ weeks first.
Inflammation re-triggers melanocytes. Less is more.
The single biggest reason melasma plans fail. Two finger-lengths, every single day, indoors and out.

supplements.name: Polypodium leucotomos 240–480 mg/d
supplements.body: Oral photoprotection. Adjunct, not replacement for SPF.

supplements.name: Tranexamic acid (oral, Rx)
supplements.body: 250 mg twice-daily, 8–12 weeks. Screened for VTE risk by your derm.

faq.a: It's controlled, not cured. The melanocytes that produce it remain hyperresponsive to UV — meaning long-term it's a maintenance condition. The good news: a strict protocol can keep it dormant for years.
It will stabilise it. To actively fade existing pigment you need an active layer (azelaic, tranexamic, or — under derm care — a Kligman-style trio). SPF determines whether your improvement holds.

faq.q: Can I do this without prescription tretinoin?
faq.a: Yes. Azelaic 10–20% twice daily, with daily SPF and tranexamic 3%, gets most patients meaningful improvement within 16 weeks.
